TextENGNew: The piece that Keiichi Maebara must first confront in order to grow into a game piece who can lead the others — a piece that is painful, agonizing, and deeply sad.
changeReason: "pitiful" (evoking pity) vs "agonizing" (evoking torment) create different emotional impressions; 辛い means agonizing/bitter, not pitiful.

TextENGNew: He must learn what it means to have companions, and learn what it means to not doubt them.
changeReason: "learn about his friends" implies gaining information about people, while "learn what it means to have companions" implies understanding the concept of companionship; 仲間 means companions/comrades.

TextENGNew: He does not yet know the weight that the words "I trust my friends" truly carry.
changeReason: Old version refers to "the importance of trusting friends"; new version specifically captures "the weight of the words 'I trust my friends'" (言葉の重さ), which is about the declaration itself, not just the concept.

TextENGNew: From the smallest seed of distrust, paranoia takes root and blooms — and Keiichi Maebara is snared by Rule X.
At the end of Rule X, Keiichi leaves behind a heartbreaking farewell letter and dies.
changeReason: "note" is generic while "farewell letter" specifies the nature of the document, closer to 遺書 (last testament/farewell letter); "sad" vs "heartbreaking" also shifts emotional intensity.

TextENGNew: It is a painful, sorrowful thing — and yet it is a necessary process for learning something truly important.
You really didn't need to apologize, you know.
In the few days he was away from Hinamizawa for a relative's funeral...
...The fact that he had his outbreak during that time is certainly tragic.
changeReason: Old version omits the temporal connection "during that time" (その間に) linking the outbreak to his absence from Hinamizawa, which is contextually important.

TextENGNew: But I think it was necessary — as his very first step toward learning something of great importance.
The first step toward drawing out Rule X and, in time, overcoming it.
Back when this was the only piece I had, I never thought of it that way, though...
